passage: When the city museum announced that it would close early due to budget cuts, the director refused to abase herself before political pressure and instead published a full report. She did not intend to abash the council, but she argued that fear should abate only when facts are public. In a tense hearing, one member tried to abbreviate her testimony, while another demanded she abdicate her post. The sharpest debate centered on a rumored art abduction that had never been fully investigated. An aberrant entry in the security log suggested someone inside the building might have chosen to abet the theft by disabling alarms. Until independent auditors reviewed the records, the board put all expansion plans in abeyance. Residents who abhor corruption packed the chamber, insisting that transparency mattered more than convenience.

questions.question: Which choice best states the central idea of the passage?

choices.A: The museum director believes public accountability should take priority over political comfort.
choices.B: The city council is justified in shortening hearings because budget cuts are unavoidable.
choices.C: The alleged art theft was definitely committed by an outside criminal group.
choices.D: Residents mainly attended the hearing to argue for expanding museum hours.

questions.explanation: The passage emphasizes the director's commitment to transparency, despite pressure and controversy.
